title: FARC Dissident Groups Launch Wave of Over 26 Attacks in Southwest Colombia
summary: FARC dissident groups carried out over twenty-six attacks including explosive incidents, armed confrontations against public officials, and roadblocks in Colombia's southwest.
contents:
# FARC Dissident Groups Launch Wave of Over 26 Attacks in Southwest Colombia

## Overview
FARC dissident groups carried out over twenty-six attacks including explosive incidents, armed confrontations against public officials, and roadblocks in Colombia's southwest. The violence resulted in twenty-one civilians killed and more than fifty-six injured. These events have disrupted stability ahead of the May 31 presidential election.

## Reporting Window
- Reported timeframe: Since April 24, 2026
- Location: Cauca, Valle del Cauca, and Nariño departments, Colombia

## Key Details
- Attacks targeted departments of Cauca, Valle del Cauca, and Nariño.
- Included explosive incidents, armed confrontations against public officials, and roadblocks.
- Perpetrated by FARC dissident groups, per Ministry of Defense.
- Caused 21 civilian deaths and over 56 injuries.
- Occurred in the lead-up to Colombia's first-round presidential election on May 31.
- Electoral environment has deteriorated in recent weeks despite largely peaceful prior elections.

## Source Context
The @CA_JC account on X (Twitter) covers CA JC's subject area as documented in this repository's source manifest. The development above appears in news reporting that aligns with the topical scope this account monitors. Verification stems from the cited publication, not from a direct tweet capture (Twitter API access remained blocked during this collection cycle).
